bullets.text: More than 500 people have been killed in election violence in Tanzania, according to an inquiry. Opposition parties have accused security forces, but the official report does not name those responsible.
Democracy is increasingly threatened by the climate crisis, with new analysis showing natural hazards disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024.
The Swiss National Bank has cut its interest rates by a half point to 0.5%.
Talks between Israel and Lebanon are underway in Washington, aiming to address decades-long disputes despite deep mistrust.

US President Trump has claimed the US has total control over the Strait of Hormuz after Iran seized two container ships, and has reportedly ordered the US Navy to destroy any Iranian mine-laying ships.
The Pentagon reportedly estimates that mine clearance in the region may take six months following the conflict.
US farmers are experiencing spiralling prices due to the Iran war, which has impacted fertiliser costs in an agricultural sector already affected by Trump’s trade war.
Oil prices are rising as Iran and Washington dispute control of the Strait of Hormuz, with Trump stating the US Navy will 'shoot and kill' any boat laying mines in the strait.
Global energy markets are on the verge of a disaster, with scenarios ranging from bad to awful due to ongoing geopolitical tensions.
